semantic.role: Automated dependency update configuration for ArchSpine, ensuring dependencies are kept current with minimal manual intervention.
responsibilities: Defining update schedules for npm and GitHub Actions package ecosystems
Setting commit message conventions (prefix, scope) for dependency PRs
Assigning labels and limiting open PRs for dependency updates
Configuring versioning strategy for npm dependencies
outOfScope: Security vulnerability alerts or automated security fixes (not configured here)
Other package ecosystems such as Docker, pip, or Maven

English.purpose: This document configures Dependabot to automatically check for and propose updates to the npm packages and GitHub Actions used by the ArchSpine project. It ensures that dependencies remain up-to-date on a weekly schedule, reducing the risk of outdated libraries and simplifying maintenance.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for maintainers and contributors of the ArchSpine repository. This file lives in the repository root and is read by GitHub's Dependabot service. Developers who merge pull requests will see automated dependency PRs with standardized commit messages and labels.
key_takeaways: Dependabot runs weekly on Monday at 09:00 Asia/Shanghai for both npm and GitHub Actions.
npm updates use the 'increase' versioning strategy and are limited to 10 open PRs.
All dependency PRs are labeled 'dependencies' and use a 'chore' commit prefix with scope included.
No other ecosystems or manual overrides are defined in this configuration.
